findings.title: Pipeline Consolidation Summary
findings.description: Consolidated 38 findings from 7 agents into 31 unique findings. Removed 7 duplicates. Identified 5 cross-agent conflicts.

findings.title: Top Priority Findings
findings.description: Highest priority items based on weighted scoring (user_impact×0.35 + business_value×0.30) / (effort×0.20 + risk×0.15): North Star Metric: Accessibility Compliance Rate (score=3.467); Color Contrast Issues Across UI Components (score=2.935); Primary KPI: Screen Reader Task Completion Rate (score=2.889); Primary KPI: Keyboard-Only Task Completion Rate (score=2.889); Accessibility Documentation Updates (score=2.786)

top_priorities.title: North Star Metric: Accessibility Compliance Rate
Color Contrast Issues Across UI Components
Primary KPI: Screen Reader Task Completion Rate
Primary KPI: Keyboard-Only Task Completion Rate

findings.title: Cross-Agent Conflict Resolution Report
findings.description: Resolved 5 conflicts between agent findings. feasibility-001 vs competitive-001: Significant accessibility gaps pose a high risk of non-compliance with WCAG 2.1 AA standards, which is a critical weakness compared to competitors.; requirements-005 vs feasibility-006: Creating a VPAT is essential for compliance and should be initiated after the MVP is completed, but the absence of a VPAT is a significant barrier for potential clients.; customer-001 vs requirements-001: The need for ARIA roles and keyboard support for visually impaired users is critical, yet existing gaps hinder navigation.

evidence.excerpt: Significant accessibility gaps pose a high risk of non-compliance with WCAG 2.1 AA standards, which is a critical weakness compared to competitors.
Creating a VPAT is essential for compliance and should be initiated after the MVP is completed, but the absence of a VPAT is a significant barrier for potential clients.
The need for ARIA roles and keyboard support for visually impaired users is critical, yet existing gaps hinder navigation.
The lack of video accessibility features is critical for users with hearing impairments, yet implementation is not prioritized.
Color contrast issues across UI components are high impact, but existing requirements to improve contrast may not be prioritized.

conflicts.description: Significant accessibility gaps pose a high risk of non-compliance with WCAG 2.1 AA standards, which is a critical weakness compared to competitors.
conflicts.resolution: Prioritize immediate remediation of accessibility gaps to achieve compliance and enhance competitive positioning.
conflicts.reasoning: Compliance with accessibility standards is essential to mitigate risks and improve market competitiveness.

conflicts.description: Creating a VPAT is essential for compliance and should be initiated after the MVP is completed, but the absence of a VPAT is a significant barrier for potential clients.
conflicts.resolution: Accelerate the VPAT creation process to coincide with MVP development to ensure compliance and attract clients.
conflicts.reasoning: Immediate action on VPAT is necessary to address compliance needs and support procurement processes.

conflicts.description: The need for ARIA roles and keyboard support for visually impaired users is critical, yet existing gaps hinder navigation.
conflicts.resolution: Implement ARIA roles and keyboard support as a priority to resolve navigation issues for visually impaired users.
conflicts.reasoning: Addressing these requirements is essential to improve user experience and accessibility compliance.

conflicts.description: The lack of video accessibility features is critical for users with hearing impairments, yet implementation is not prioritized.
conflicts.resolution: Prioritize the implementation of video accessibility features, including captions and transcripts, to meet user needs.
conflicts.reasoning: Enhancing video accessibility is crucial for compliance and user satisfaction.

conflicts.description: Color contrast issues across UI components are high impact, but existing requirements to improve contrast may not be prioritized.
conflicts.resolution: Prioritize color contrast improvements in the MVP to ensure compliance and enhance readability for users with visual impairments.
conflicts.reasoning: Addressing color contrast is essential for meeting accessibility standards and improving user experience.

summary: Consolidated 38 findings into 31 unique items. Removed 7 duplicates, resolved 5 conflicts. Top priority: North Star Metric: Accessibility Compliance Rate.
